passage_title: Return of the elm: reintroducing the beloved tree to Britain

sentences.sentence: Around 25 million elms, accounting for 90% of all elm trees in the UK, died during the 1960s and '70s from Dutch elm disease.
sentences.translation: 约有2500万棵榆树，即英国全部榆树的90%，在20世纪60年代和70年代因荷兰榆树病而死亡。

structure.text: accounting for 90% of all elm trees in the UK

difficult_points: accounting for... 是现在分词短语，补充说明2500万棵榆树所占比例。
from Dutch elm disease 表示死亡原因。
the 1960s and '70s 中 '70s 省略了 19，指20世纪70年代。

sentences.sentence: In the aftermath, the elm, once so dominant in the British landscape, was largely forgotten.
sentences.translation: 在这场灾害之后，曾经在英国景观中占据主导地位的榆树基本被人遗忘了。

structure.text: once so dominant in the British landscape

difficult_points: In the aftermath 指荷兰榆树病造成大规模死亡之后。
once so dominant in the British landscape 是插入性补充，说明榆树过去的重要地位。
was largely forgotten 是被动语态，表示榆树在人们关注中淡出。

sentences.sentence: However, there's now hope the elm may be reintroduced to the countryside of central and southern England.
sentences.translation: 然而，如今人们有希望把榆树重新引入英格兰中部和南部的乡村地区。

structure.text: the elm may be reintroduced to the countryside of central and southern England

difficult_points: However 表示与上一句“被遗忘”形成转折。
there's now hope 后省略 that，引出具体希望的内容。
may be reintroduced 是被动结构，表示榆树可能被重新引入。

sentences.sentence: Any reintroduction will start from a very low base.
sentences.translation: 任何重新引入都将从非常低的基础开始。
difficult_points: Any reintroduction 概括所有可能的榆树回归计划。
from a very low base 表示现有成熟榆树数量少，起点很低。

sentences.sentence: 'The impact of the disease is difficult to picture if you hadn't seen what was there before,' says Matt Elliot of the Woodland Trust.
sentences.translation: 林地信托基金会的 Matt Elliot 说：“如果你没见过以前那里的景象，就很难想象这种疾病的影响。”

structure.text: The impact of the disease is difficult to picture
if you hadn't seen what was there before
says Matt Elliot of the Woodland Trust

difficult_points: if 引导条件从句，说明难以想象影响的前提。
what was there before 是宾语从句，指疾病发生前的榆树景观。
hadn't seen 是过去完成时，强调在回顾时此前未曾见过。

sentences.sentence: 'You look at old photographs from the 1960s and it's only then that you realise the impact [elms had] ... They were significant, large trees ... then they were gone.'
sentences.translation: “你看看20世纪60年代的老照片，只有到那时你才会意识到榆树曾产生的影响……它们曾是重要的大树……后来它们就没了。”

structure.text: You look at old photographs from the 1960s
and it's only then that you realise the impact [elms had]

difficult_points: it's only then that... 是强调句，突出看到旧照片之后才意识到影响。
[elms had] 是编辑补充，说明 impact 的施动者是 elms。
省略号表示说话者停顿，强调榆树从显著存在到消失的落差。

sentences.sentence: The disease is caused by a fungus that blocks the elms' vascular (water, nutrient and food transport) system, causing branches to wilt and die.
sentences.translation: 这种疾病由一种真菌引起，这种真菌会阻塞榆树的维管系统（输送水分、养分和食物的系统），导致树枝枯萎死亡。

structure.text: that blocks the elms' vascular (water, nutrient and food transport) system
causing branches to wilt and die

difficult_points: that 引导定语从句，修饰 a fungus。
括号内容解释 vascular system 的功能。
causing... 是现在分词结果状语，说明阻塞系统后的后果。

sentences.sentence: A first epidemic, which occurred in the 1920s, gradually died down, but in the '70s a second epidemic was triggered by shipments of elm from Canada.
sentences.translation: 第一次流行病发生在20世纪20年代，后来逐渐平息；但在70年代，来自加拿大的榆木运输引发了第二次流行病。

structure.text: but in the '70s a second epidemic was triggered by shipments of elm from Canada

difficult_points: which occurred in the 1920s 是非限制性定语从句，补充第一次流行病的时间。
died down 表示疫情逐渐减弱、平息。
was triggered by 是被动结构，说明第二次流行病的诱因。

sentences.sentence: The wood came in the form of logs destined for boat building and its intact bark was perfect for the elm bark beetles that spread the deadly fungus.
sentences.translation: 这些木材以用于造船的原木形式运来，而完整的树皮非常适合传播致命真菌的榆树皮甲虫。

structure.text: The wood came in the form of logs destined for boat building
and its intact bark was perfect for the elm bark beetles

difficult_points: destined for boat building 修饰 logs，表示这些原木的用途。
its intact bark 中 its 指 The wood。
that spread the deadly fungus 修饰 elm bark beetles，说明甲虫的危害。

sentences.sentence: This time, the beetles carried a much more virulent strain that destroyed the vast majority of British elms.
sentences.translation: 这一次，甲虫携带了一种毒性强得多的菌株，摧毁了绝大多数英国榆树。

structure.text: carried a much more virulent strain
that destroyed the vast majority of British elms

difficult_points: This time 对比20世纪20年代的第一次流行病。
much more virulent 表示该菌株毒性强得多。
that 引导定语从句，修饰 strain。

sentences.sentence: Today, elms still exist in the southern English countryside but mostly only in low hedgerows between fields.
sentences.translation: 如今，榆树仍存在于英格兰南部乡村，但大多只出现在田地之间低矮的树篱中。

structure.text: elms still exist in the southern English countryside
but mostly only in low hedgerows between fields

difficult_points: still exist 表示榆树并未完全消失。
but mostly only 限定其存在范围，说明多为低矮树篱。
between fields 修饰 hedgerows，说明位置。

sentences.sentence: 'We have millions of small elms in hedgerows but they get targeted by the beetle as soon as they reach a certain size,' says Karen Russell, co-author of the report 'Where we are with elm'.
sentences.translation: 《榆树现状》报告的合著者 Karen Russell 说：“树篱中有数百万棵小榆树，但它们一长到一定大小就会成为甲虫的目标。”

structure.text: We have millions of small elms in hedgerows
but they get targeted by the beetle
as soon as they reach a certain size
co-author of the report 'Where we are with elm'

difficult_points: as soon as 引导时间状语从句，表示“一……就……”。
get targeted 是被动含义，说明小榆树会被甲虫攻击。
co-author... 是 Karen Russell 的同位语，说明其身份。

sentences.sentence: Once the trunk of the elm reaches 10–15 centimetres or so in diameter, it becomes a perfect size for beetles to lay eggs and for the fungus to take hold.
sentences.translation: 一旦榆树树干直径达到约10到15厘米，它就会成为甲虫产卵和真菌扎根的理想大小。

structure.text: Once the trunk of the elm reaches 10–15 centimetres or so in diameter
and for the fungus to take hold

difficult_points: Once 引导条件/时间从句，说明榆树达到某直径后会发生什么。
it 指 the trunk of the elm 或榆树达到的尺寸状态。
for...to... 结构分别说明甲虫和真菌适合利用这一尺寸。

sentences.sentence: Yet mature specimens have been identified, in counties such as Cambridgeshire, that are hundreds of years old, and have mysteriously escaped the epidemic.
sentences.translation: 然而，在剑桥郡等郡，人们已经发现了一些成熟树种样本，它们已有数百年树龄，并神秘地躲过了这场流行病。

structure.text: Yet mature specimens have been identified
that are hundreds of years old
and have mysteriously escaped the epidemic

difficult_points: Yet 表示转折：多数榆树易受攻击，但仍有成熟个体幸存。
that are hundreds of years old 修饰 mature specimens。
have mysteriously escaped 使用现在完成时，强调这些树至今仍幸存。

sentences.sentence: The key, Russell says, is to identify and study those trees that have survived and work out why they stood tall when millions of others succumbed.
sentences.translation: Russell 说，关键是找出并研究那些存活下来的树，弄清为什么在数百万棵其他榆树倒下时它们依然挺立。

structure.text: is to identify and study those trees
why they stood tall when millions of others succumbed

difficult_points: Russell says 是插入语，主干是 The key is to identify...。
those trees that have survived 中 that 从句限定幸存的树。
why 引导宾语从句，说明研究的目标是找出幸存原因。

sentences.sentence: Nevertheless, opportunities are limited as the number of these mature survivors is relatively small.
sentences.translation: 尽管如此，由于这些成熟幸存树的数量相对较少，研究机会也很有限。

structure.text: as the number of these mature survivors is relatively small

difficult_points: Nevertheless 表示承接前文研究价值后转向限制。
as 引导原因状语从句，说明机会有限的原因。
the number of... 作主语时谓语用单数 is。

sentences.sentence: 'What are the reasons for their survival?' asks Russell.
sentences.translation: Russell 问道：“它们存活下来的原因是什么？”

structure.text: What are the reasons for their survival?

difficult_points: What 引导直接问句，询问幸存原因。
their survival 中 their 指成熟幸存榆树的存活。

sentences.sentence: 'Avoidance, tolerance, resistance?
sentences.translation: “是避开了病害、耐受病害，还是具有抗性？
difficult_points: 这是省略问句，完整含义是询问幸存原因属于哪一种机制。
三个名词并列，分别表示避开、耐受和抵抗病害。

sentences.sentence: We don't know where the balance lies between the three.
sentences.translation: 我们不知道这三者之间的比重在哪里。

structure.text: where the balance lies between the three

difficult_points: where 引导宾语从句，作 know 的宾语。
the three 指上一句列出的 avoidance、tolerance 和 resistance。
where the balance lies 表示三种因素的相对重要性尚不清楚。

sentences.sentence: I don't see how it can be entirely down to luck.'
sentences.translation: 我看不出这怎么可能完全归因于运气。”

structure.text: how it can be entirely down to luck

difficult_points: how 引导宾语从句，表示“如何可能”。
it 指榆树幸存这一现象。
be down to luck 表示归因于运气，entirely 强调完全归因。

sentences.sentence: For centuries, elm ran a close second to oak as the hardwood tree of choice in Britain and was in many instances the most prominent tree in the landscape.
sentences.translation: 数百年来，榆树作为英国首选硬木树种的地位仅次于橡树，并且在许多情况下是景观中最显眼的树。

structure.text: elm ran a close second to oak
as the hardwood tree of choice in Britain
and was in many instances the most prominent tree in the landscape

difficult_points: ran a close second to oak 表示地位紧随橡树之后。
as the hardwood tree of choice 说明比较的身份或类别。
was 与 ran 共用主语 elm，构成并列谓语。

sentences.sentence: Not only was elm common in European forests, it became a key component of birch, ash and hazel woodlands.
sentences.translation: 榆树不仅在欧洲森林中很常见，还成为桦树、梣树和榛树林地的重要组成部分。

structure.text: Not only was elm common in European forests
it became a key component of birch, ash and hazel woodlands
of birch, ash and hazel woodlands

difficult_points: Not only 置于句首引起倒装，was 位于主语 elm 前。
it 指 elm。
a key component of 表示榆树是这些林地的重要组成。

sentences.sentence: The use of elm is thought to go back to the Bronze Age, when it was widely used for tools.
sentences.translation: 人们认为榆木的使用可追溯到青铜时代，当时它被广泛用于制作工具。

structure.text: is thought to go back to the Bronze Age
when it was widely used for tools

difficult_points: is thought to 表示“被认为”，弱化断言语气。
go back to 表示历史可追溯到某时期。
when 引导非限制性定语从句，补充说明青铜时代的使用情况。

sentences.sentence: Elm was also the preferred material for shields and early swords.
sentences.translation: 榆木也是盾牌和早期剑具的首选材料。

structure.text: the preferred material for shields and early swords

difficult_points: also 承接上一句工具用途，进一步补充榆木的用途。
preferred material 表示优先选择的材料。

sentences.sentence: In the 18th century, it was planted more widely and its wood was used for items such as storage crates and flooring.
sentences.translation: 在18世纪，榆树被更广泛地种植，其木材被用于储物箱和地板等物品。

structure.text: and its wood was used for items such as storage crates and flooring
such as storage crates and flooring

difficult_points: it 指 elm。
was planted 和 was used 都是被动语态，描述人们对榆树的利用。
such as 后列举 items 的具体例子。

sentences.sentence: It was also suitable for items that experienced high levels of impact and was used to build the keel of the 19th-century sailing ship Cutty Sark as well as mining equipment.
sentences.translation: 榆木还适合制作承受高强度冲击的物品，并被用于建造19世纪帆船“卡蒂萨克号”的龙骨以及采矿设备。

structure.text: It was also suitable for items
that experienced high levels of impact
and was used to build the keel of the 19th-century sailing ship Cutty Sark as well as mining equipment

difficult_points: It 指 Elm 或榆木。
that experienced high levels of impact 修饰 items，说明这些物品承受高冲击。
as well as 连接 keel 和 mining equipment 两类用途。

sentences.sentence: Given how ingrained elm is in British culture, it's unsurprising the tree has many advocates.
sentences.translation: 考虑到榆树在英国文化中根深蒂固，这种树拥有许多支持者也就不足为奇了。

structure.text: Given how ingrained elm is in British culture
how ingrained elm is in British culture

difficult_points: Given 表示“考虑到”，后接榆树文化地位这一背景。
it 是形式主语，真正内容是 the tree has many advocates。
ingrained 表示深深扎根于文化之中。

sentences.sentence: Amongst them is Peter Bourne of the National Elm Collection in Brighton.
sentences.translation: 布莱顿国家榆树收藏馆的 Peter Bourne 就是这些支持者之一。

structure.text: Peter Bourne of the National Elm Collection in Brighton

difficult_points: Amongst them 置于句首，引出 advocates 中的一个例子。
该句是倒装结构，真正主语是 Peter Bourne。
of the National Elm Collection in Brighton 说明 Peter Bourne 的机构归属。

sentences.sentence: 'I saw Dutch elm disease unfold as a small boy,' he says.
sentences.translation: 他说：“我小时候亲眼看到荷兰榆树病逐渐蔓延。”

structure.text: I saw Dutch elm disease unfold

difficult_points: saw...unfold 表示亲眼看到事件逐渐展开。
as a small boy 说明说话者见到这一过程时的年龄阶段。
Dutch elm disease unfold 中 unfold 强调疾病发展过程。

sentences.sentence: 'The elm seemed to be part of rural England, but I remember watching trees just lose their leaves and that really stayed with me.'
sentences.translation: “榆树似乎曾是英格兰乡村的一部分，但我记得看到树木就那样掉光叶子，那一幕真的一直留在我心里。”

structure.text: The elm seemed to be part of rural England
but I remember watching trees just lose their leaves
watching trees just lose their leaves
and that really stayed with me

difficult_points: seemed to be 表示在说话者记忆中榆树似乎构成乡村的一部分。
remember watching 后接动名词短语，表示记得看见这一过程。
that 指看到树木失去叶子的经历。

sentences.sentence: Today, the city of Brighton's elms total about 17,000.
sentences.translation: 如今，布莱顿市的榆树总数约为17000棵。
difficult_points: the city of Brighton's elms 表示布莱顿市拥有的榆树。
total 在这里是动词，表示总数达到。

sentences.sentence: Local factors appear to have contributed to their survival.
sentences.translation: 当地因素似乎对这些榆树的存活起了作用。
difficult_points: appear to 表示推测语气，不是确定断言。
have contributed to 表示这些因素已经对存活产生影响。
their survival 指布莱顿榆树的存活。

sentences.sentence: Strong winds from the sea make it difficult for the determined elm bark beetle to attack this coastal city's elm population.
sentences.translation: 来自海上的强风使顽强的榆树皮甲虫难以攻击这座沿海城市的榆树群。

structure.text: for the determined elm bark beetle to attack this coastal city's elm population
to attack this coastal city's elm population

difficult_points: make it difficult for...to... 中 it 是形式宾语，真正内容是不定式短语。
determined 形容甲虫难以阻挡或持续进攻的特点。
this coastal city's elm population 指布莱顿的榆树种群。

sentences.sentence: However, the situation is precarious.
sentences.translation: 然而，形势仍然不稳定。
difficult_points: However 表示与前一句的有利海风因素形成转折。
precarious 表示局面不稳、仍有风险。

sentences.sentence: 'The beetles can just march in if we're not careful, as the threat is right on our doorstep,' says Bourne.
sentences.translation: Bourne 说：“如果我们不小心，甲虫就会直接大举进入，因为威胁就在我们家门口。”

structure.text: The beetles can just march in
as the threat is right on our doorstep

difficult_points: if 引导条件状语从句，说明甲虫入侵的条件。
as 引导原因状语从句，解释为什么必须小心。
right on our doorstep 是比喻表达，表示威胁近在咫尺。

sentences.sentence: Any prospect of the elm returning relies heavily on trees being either resistant to, or tolerant of, the disease.
sentences.translation: 榆树回归的任何前景，都在很大程度上依赖于树木对这种疾病具有抗性或耐受性。

structure.text: Any prospect of the elm returning
trees being either resistant to, or tolerant of, the disease

difficult_points: of the elm returning 是动名词复合结构，修饰 prospect。
being either resistant to, or tolerant of, the disease 是 on 的宾语。
resistant to 和 tolerant of 区分“抗病”和“耐受”。

sentences.sentence: This means a widespread reintroduction would involve existing or new hybrid strains derived from resistant, generally non-native elm species.
sentences.translation: 这意味着大范围重新引入将涉及现有或新的杂交品系，这些品系来源于具有抗性的、通常非本土的榆树物种。

structure.text: a widespread reintroduction would involve existing or new hybrid strains
derived from resistant, generally non-native elm species

difficult_points: This 指上一句关于抗性或耐受性的前提。
would involve 表示如果进行大范围重新引入，将需要使用这些品系。
derived from... 是过去分词短语，说明 hybrid strains 的来源。

sentences.sentence: A new generation of seedlings have been bred and tested to see if they can withstand the fungus by cutting a small slit in the bark and injecting a tiny amount of the pathogen.
sentences.translation: 新一代幼苗已经被培育并测试，方法是在树皮上切开一个小口并注入少量病原体，以观察它们是否能够抵御这种真菌。

structure.text: to see if they can withstand the fungus
if they can withstand the fungus
by cutting a small slit in the bark and injecting a tiny amount of the pathogen

difficult_points: have been bred and tested 是现在完成被动结构，说明幼苗已被培育和测试。
to see if... 表示测试目的。
by cutting...and injecting... 说明测试方法。

sentences.sentence: 'The effects are very quick,' says Russell.
sentences.translation: Russell 说：“效果会很快显现。”
difficult_points: The effects 指注入病原体后的反应。
very quick 表示测试结果出现得很快。

sentences.sentence: 'You return in four to six weeks and trees that are resistant show no symptoms, whereas those that are susceptible show leaf loss and may even have died completely.'
sentences.translation: “四到六周后你再回来，具有抗性的树没有任何症状，而易感的树会出现落叶，甚至可能已经完全死亡。”

structure.text: You return in four to six weeks
and trees that are resistant show no symptoms
whereas those that are susceptible show leaf loss and may even have died completely

difficult_points: that are resistant 修饰 trees，说明无症状的是抗病树。
whereas 表示对比，比较抗病树与易感树的不同反应。
may even have died completely 使用完成式，表示到观察时可能已经死亡。

sentences.sentence: All of this raises questions of social acceptance, acknowledges Russell.
sentences.translation: Russell 承认，所有这些都会引发社会接受度的问题。
difficult_points: All of this 指前文使用抗病或非本土杂交品系的方案。
raises questions of 表示引发有关某问题的疑问。
acknowledges Russell 是倒装式报告语。

sentences.sentence: 'If we're putting elm back into the landscape, a small element of it is not native – are we bothered about that?'
sentences.translation: “如果我们把榆树重新放回景观中，而其中有一小部分并非本土物种，我们会在意这一点吗？”

structure.text: If we're putting elm back into the landscape
a small element of it is not native

difficult_points: If 引导条件从句，设定重新引入榆树的情境。
a small element of it 指重新引入方案中少量非本土成分。
are we bothered about that? 是反问式问题，询问社会是否介意非本土成分。

sentences.sentence: For her, the environmental case for reintroducing elm is strong.
sentences.translation: 在她看来，重新引入榆树在环境方面的理由很充分。

structure.text: the environmental case for reintroducing elm

difficult_points: For her 表示从 Russell 的角度看。
case 在这里不是“案例”，而是支持某做法的理由。
for reintroducing elm 修饰 environmental case，说明理由针对重新引入榆树。

sentences.sentence: 'They will host wildlife, which is a good thing.'
sentences.translation: “它们会为野生动物提供栖息环境，这是一件好事。”
difficult_points: They 指重新引入的榆树。
host wildlife 表示为野生动物提供栖息或寄居环境。
which 指整件事，即榆树支持野生动物。

expressions.meaning_zh: 为野生动物提供栖息环境

sentences.sentence: Others are more wary.
sentences.translation: 其他人则更为谨慎。
difficult_points: Others 指不同于 Russell 的其他相关人士。
more wary 表示他们对重新引入方案更谨慎或有所保留。

sentences.sentence: 'On the face of it, it seems like a good idea,' says Elliot.
sentences.translation: Elliot 说：“表面上看，这似乎是个好主意。”

structure.text: it seems like a good idea

difficult_points: On the face of it 表示“表面看来”，暗示后面会提出问题。
it 指重新引入榆树的想法。
seems like 表示初步判断，而非最终认可。

sentences.sentence: The problem, he suggests, is that, 'You're replacing a native species with a horticultural analogue*.
sentences.translation: 他指出，问题在于：“你是在用一种园艺类似种替代本土物种。

structure.text: You're replacing a native species with a horticultural analogue*

difficult_points: he suggests 是插入语，说明问题判断来自 Elliot。
that 后接直接引语形式的表语内容。
replace...with... 表示用园艺类似种替代本土物种，这是他担忧的核心。

sentences.sentence: You're effectively cloning.'
sentences.translation: 你实际上是在克隆。”
difficult_points: effectively 表示从实际效果上看，而非严格技术意义上的完全克隆。
cloning 延续上一句对使用园艺类似种的批评。

sentences.sentence: There's also the risk of introducing new diseases.
sentences.translation: 此外，还存在引入新疾病的风险。

structure.text: the risk of introducing new diseases

difficult_points: There is 结构表示存在某种风险。
also 表明这是除替代本土物种之外的另一项担忧。
of introducing new diseases 修饰 risk，说明风险内容。

sentences.sentence: Rather than plant new elms, the Woodland Trust emphasises providing space to those elms that have survived independently.
sentences.translation: 林地信托基金会强调，与其种植新的榆树，不如为那些独立幸存下来的榆树提供空间。

structure.text: emphasises providing space to those elms

difficult_points: Rather than plant new elms 表示与种植新榆树相比更倾向另一做法。
providing space 是 emphasises 的宾语，说明强调的行动。
that have survived independently 修饰 those elms，指自然幸存的榆树。

sentences.sentence: 'Sometimes the best thing you can do is just give nature time to recover ... over time, you might get resistance,' says Elliot.
sentences.translation: Elliot 说：“有时候，你能做的最好的事就是给自然时间去恢复……随着时间推移，你也许会看到抗性出现。”

structure.text: Sometimes the best thing you can do is just give nature time to recover
over time, you might get resistance

difficult_points: you can do 修饰 the best thing，说明“你能做的事”。
give nature time to recover 表示让自然恢复，而非主动大规模干预。
might get resistance 表示随着时间可能出现抗性，但并不确定。

sentences.sentence: * horticultural analogue: a cultivated plant species that is genetically similar to an existing species
sentences.translation: ＊horticultural analogue：一种栽培植物物种，在基因上与某个现有物种相似。

structure.text: that is genetically similar to an existing species

difficult_points: 这是对正文中 horticultural analogue 的词汇注释，属于源段落文本。
that 引导定语从句，解释这种栽培植物物种的特征。
genetically similar to 强调基因层面的相似，而不是完全相同。
